title: Resetting your account password
text: Customers can reset a forgotten password from the sign in page by selecting Forgot password and entering the email address on the account. A secure reset link is sent within a few minutes. The link expires after thirty minutes and can be used only once. If the email does not arrive, customers should check spam folders, confirm that the address is spelled correctly, and verify that single sign on is not required by their organization. Support agents should never ask for the old password or send a temporary password over chat.

title: Setting up multi factor authentication
text: Multi factor authentication adds a second verification step when users sign in. Users can enable it from Security settings by choosing an authenticator app, SMS, or a hardware security key. Authenticator apps are recommended because they work without cellular service and are less vulnerable to SIM swap attacks. Recovery codes should be downloaded and stored in a safe place before leaving the setup screen. If a user loses all second factors, an administrator can start account recovery after verifying identity.

title: Downloading invoices and receipts
text: Billing administrators can download invoices from the Billing history page. Each invoice includes the billing period, payment method, tax details, purchase order number when provided, and a list of plan charges. Receipts are available after a payment succeeds. If an invoice is missing, confirm that the user has billing administrator permissions and that the workspace is not managed by a reseller. Accounting teams can configure invoice emails so monthly statements are sent to a shared finance inbox.

title: Refunds, credits, and cancellations
text: Refund eligibility depends on the subscription plan and the timing of the cancellation request. Monthly plans can be canceled at any time and remain active through the end of the paid billing period. Annual contracts are usually not refundable after the renewal date, but unused seats may qualify for account credit in some regions. Customers should include the workspace ID, invoice number, and reason for the request when contacting billing support. Downgrades take effect at the next renewal unless required by local law.

title: Search export limits and supported formats
text: Search results can be exported as CSV, JSON, or Excel files from the results toolbar. Standard workspaces can export up to ten thousand matching records per job. Enterprise workspaces can request larger exports through the admin console. Exports include document ID, title, matched fields, timestamp, owner, and selected metadata. Very large exports are processed asynchronously and send an email notification when the file is ready. Export links expire after seven days for security reasons.

title: Using filters in document search
text: Document search supports filters for owner, file type, date range, status, language, and custom metadata. Filters can be combined with quoted phrases or natural language queries. A date filter narrows results to documents created or modified during the selected range. The owner filter matches the current document owner, not the original creator. When results look incomplete, users should clear saved filters and verify that they have permission to view the target documents.

title: Configuring Slack notifications
text: Slack notifications can be configured from Integrations by connecting a workspace and selecting the channels that should receive alerts. Users may create rules for failed imports, completed exports, mention activity, billing events, or security warnings. Private channels require the installer to be a member of the channel before it appears in the list. If alerts stop arriving, reconnect the Slack app, review channel permissions, and confirm that notification rules are still enabled.

title: Creating and rotating API keys
text: Developers can create API keys from the Developer settings page. Each key should be named for the service that uses it and assigned the smallest set of scopes needed for the integration. API keys are shown only once at creation time, so they should be copied into a secure secret manager immediately. Keys can be rotated by creating a replacement key, updating the application configuration, verifying traffic, and then revoking the old key. Never commit API keys to source control.

title: Data retention and deletion windows
text: Workspace owners can configure retention policies that automatically delete documents after a selected number of days. Retention applies to active documents, archived documents, and most derived search indexes. Deleted records enter a recovery window before permanent removal. Legal holds override automated deletion until the hold is released. Audit logs keep a separate retention schedule so security teams can investigate access events even after the underlying document has expired.

title: Audit log events and investigation workflow
text: Audit logs record important account and workspace activity such as sign ins, password changes, multi factor authentication updates, API key creation, exports, permission changes, and document deletion. Security administrators can filter audit logs by actor, event type, IP address, date range, and affected resource. Logs can be exported to a SIEM system through the events API. During an investigation, compare unusual activity with user travel, recent admin changes, and known automation jobs.

title: Offline mode in the mobile app
text: The mobile app can make selected documents available offline. Users should open a document and tap Make available offline while connected to WiFi. Offline documents are encrypted on the device and refresh when the app reconnects. Search in offline mode is limited to downloaded content and may not include the newest workspace changes. Administrators can disable offline storage for regulated teams from the mobile device policy page.

title: Troubleshooting mobile push notifications
text: Mobile push notifications depend on device permissions, workspace notification settings, and operating system background delivery. If alerts do not appear, users should confirm that notifications are enabled in the app, verify that quiet hours are not active, and check the device level permission screen. Reinstalling the app refreshes the push token when a device has stopped receiving alerts. Enterprise device managers may also block notifications for managed profiles.
